mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes on AWS. It is ideal for organizations looking for a robust, scalable solution without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
The AWS CLI is required to interact with AWS services and create the EKS cluster.

```bash

pip install awscli --upgrade --user

```

> Ensure you have Python and pip installed.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count in a designated region.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID and subnet IDs with your actual values.

steps.title: Update kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command updates your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to interact with your new EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster

```

> This sets up the kubeconfig for your EKS cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your EKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see your nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your EKS cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/metrics-server/master/deploy/1.8+/metrics-server-deployment.yaml

```

> This sets up the cluster autoscaler.
> This installs the metrics server for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster

```

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
After deleting the cluster, ensure to manually delete any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?contains(LoadBalancerName, `my-cluster`)].LoadBalancerArn'

```

> List any load balancers associated with the cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster resources and configurations. Then, perform the upgrade and verify the status.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources.
> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that the nodes are updated and in 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the EKS cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or if IAM permissions are insufficient.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster' and ensure your IAM user has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the nodes are not properly registered or if there are networking issues.

**Fix:**
Check the EC2 instance status and security group settings. Ensure the nodes can communicate with the control plane.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster autoscaler not scaling up
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster autoscaler may not have the correct IAM permissions or configuration.

**Fix:**
Ensure the IAM role associated with the cluster has the necessary permissions for autoscaling.

troubleshooting.title: Metrics server not available
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The metrics server may not be deployed correctly or may have insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the metrics server pod and ensure it is running with the correct RBAC permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved integration with AWS services.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to review the new IAM permissions required for the metrics server.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the AWS CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
